blocks.content: Dean came in from the porch. He looked at Nadia at the stove, at Cade at the counter, at Wren on the couch, and did the quiet inventory of a man who'd been watching people his whole life.

blocks.speaker: Dean
blocks.content: Davenport reached her last night. She already knew. I just — I needed to call her. I needed her to hear a voice she recognized.

blocks.speaker: Dean
blocks.content: She's — she's his mother. She kept asking me what happened and I didn't have an answer. She asked me to find out. She said find out what happened to him.
His voice broke on the last word. He pressed his mouth together. Held it.
She crossed the room and put her arms around him from behind and held on and Dean let her, one hand over hers.
Cade looked at the ceiling. Blinked.
Nadia turned back to the stove. Her jaw was tight. Her eyes were dry.
